title: 141 Keys Botanical Keycaps Cherry Profile PBT Dye Sub Keycap Set Suitable for 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches Mechanical Gaming Keyboard
features: 【PBT Keycaps】These 141 cherry profile keycaps are made of thick PBT material, with a matte texture and anti-grease coating, which can provide good durability and typing.

【Cherry Profile】This set of pbt keycaps uses the cherry height, which is higher than the XDA profile. It's ergonomically designed, so you will not feel tired even if you type or play games for a long time.
【Rugged and Durable】The thickness of these keycaps are about 1.4~1.5mm, so the keycaps are very durable. The thick PBT material will effectively extend the service life of the keycaps.

【Wide Selection】These keycaps are suitable for most keyboards, such as 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 cherry mx switches mechanical Keyboards.If you are not sure whether this keycaps set is fit for your keyboard, please consult customer service.
【Warm Tips】This product is a set of keycaps, not a keyboard. If the keycaps are missing or cracked, don't worry, we'll send you new replacement keys.

reviews.title: OMG great keycap set!
reviews.text:
I have been looking for these sets for the longest time, outside of GB and these guys have made a solid replica. i am so impressed with the quality and feel of these Botanical keycap sets that the premium sets can remain on the shelf. They look so nice and very very close and clean to the originals.

the only way you can tell that they are not GMK is by looking at the bottom. The dye-sub is a little weak in the finish but you never look under the hood.

If you like the greenery landscape or the color great fanatic this is the best set. It's super clean, subtle, and sounds great! I have this set on my KBD67 Lite and it sounds so much better than my ABS keycaps i paid $80 for. The texture is also very nice and I seem to be keen on this rough like texture than the ABS smoother feel. It kind of is matte if that makes sense. The legends on all the alphas are uniform, no thickness issues. The ,.? and other modifiers might have a thinner font but negligible.

I can't say enough what a great first impression these sets have given me and if they produce an "Oblivian" replica i'm jumping on it as well. Pictures to come, after I pickup my desk from all the loose keycaps.

reviews.title: Great sound
reviews.text: Don’t wait years for a gmk set these are just as good lol

title: Hexgears Impulse - Hot-swap Mechanical Keyboard (Box Brown Switches, Pudding Keycaps)
description:
The Hexgears Impulse is available in many amazing configurations.
— Durable PBT Keycaps
PBT is an engineering plastic that resists shine and finger grease. This is far better than the cheap plastic used by major brands. All Impulses use PBT in their doubleshot injection molded keycaps — the main letters won’t ever wear off.
— Awesome RGB Backlighting and RGB Ring Lighting
The Impulse lighting system is on-board, ensuring compatibility with various operating systems. Translucent pudding keycaps showcase its smooth RGB lighting in a way that other keyboards can only dream of. RGB customization options allow you to select an experience that matches your battlestation.
— Survives Your Worst Impulses
Do you have a coffee habit? A cat that spills things on the regular? The Impulse handles liquids like a champ. IP56 water resistance means that the keyboard functions while blasted with water jets. Its curved metal frame is also quite rigid.
— Kailh Box Switches
Kailh Box switches are rated for 80 million presses — way above industry standard. They have drainage ports and self-cleaning housings. Box switches also feel excellent, as they’re less wobbly and noticeably smoother than standard models. Tactile Box switches result in relatively quiet mechanical keyboards, but the clicky versions go all out with a new and improved mechanism.
— Other Features
The Impulse is designed as a plug and play device. Its feature set offers exactly what you need to get up and running fast. 2-stage angle adjustment feet and basic onboard functions like Windows key locking enhance your experience.
Impulse keyboards have a 1-year limited warranty.
features: RGB, Per-key customizable lighting modes, RGB Light Ring
Two-shot extra durable PBT keycaps (Pudding & 2-tone)
Curved metal mounting plate with 2 stage adjustable height feet
IP56 water resistant design, N-key Rollover (NKRO), integrated USB-A cable
Available in Kailh BOX Brown (Tactile), BOX White (Clicky), and Hako Clear (Heavy Tactile) Mechanical Switches
Updated Impulse V2 design with Hot-swap switch sockets (MX-compatible, LED compatible, plate mount)

reviews.title: Good Keyboard, clicky, and positive return
reviews.text:
Hi, I am a bit of a keyboard junky. I have bought probably 10 mechanical keyboards over the last 3 years. I still have an original IBM PC keyboard - that is really stiff and loud. But about this keyboard, I read an online review and said, why not - and I have been using it for several days. First, it sets up easy. Just plug in to get started. Configuring all the lights is more work and not completely intuitive - especially if you lose the little manual 3x4" or so. Personally, I don't care that much for the lighting features on any keyboard - I want to know if I can type quickly without any errors from the keyboard as opposed from me. In general, this keyboard is one of the best. The key depression effort is light to medium - if you rest your fingers on the keys, they will not trigger -which is good. The space bar works well - and I usually find this a problem because I hit it a bit off center - but this one works well. The noise is medium. The clacky sound is the sound of me pressing the keys to the maximum and not the click of the membrane.

I generally like this keyboard and I have tried many from $79 mechanical keys to $200 keyboards. I think this is up there with the best - maybe not all the bells and whistles like volume control, but I never use that. Buy this. Works well. Feels good under the fingers. Type fast!

reviews.title: Recommended for Gaming
reviews.text: Nice keyboard. Has NKRO, macro support, and lots of lighting patterns for the keyboard and ring lighting. Looks very nice in the Pudding keycaps version with full RGB. I ordered it with the Kailh Box Brown switches, which work smoothly, and have a somewhat subtle tactile feedback. I tested it fully, and there was no key chatter detected. Well worth the reasonable asking price and one of the very few current options offering these switches in a commercial build.

reviews.title: Great for typing
reviews.text:
I have the version with white keycaps and "box white" clicky switches. I don't play computer games, so I can't comment on that aspect of the keyboard. But I can say that this is a very good typist's keyboard. The key switches sound like Cherry Blues, but I think these feel smoother, and have less lateral wiggle room.

The keyboard comes with a tiny "user guide" that is no more than a list of keystrokes that control the lighting and macros. I don't care for lighting on a keyboard, so the first thing I tried to do was dim the lights. The "user guide" wasn't very clear on that, so here's how:

While holding down the "Fn" key...
... press the Down arrow to dim the white lights.
... press the "2" key on the numeric keypad to dim the colored perimeter lights.

Keep pressing those keys to dim the lights further. I've now got them completely dimmed, and I hope they stay that way.

One thing to know about this keyboard is that the keycaps aren't recessed. On most keyboards, you can't see or grasp the bottoms of the keycaps, because they fit into cavities formed by the molded plastic deck. This keyboard's top deck is flat metal, without cavities. So there's about a quarter-inch gap between the keycap bottoms and the top of the deck. As a result, it's easy to pull the keycaps. You can even remove the Escape key with your bare fingers. I don't think this is a bad thing; it's just worth knowing about.

Speaking of keycaps, these use a stylized font that sacrifices legibility for fashion. The designer apparently dislikes closed shapes, and created little vertical gaps in the Q, O, zero, 8, etc. Not even the ampersand, pound sign, or the little orbs of the percent sign are spared this annoying treatment. It looks like the dye is flaking away. As a sometimes hunt-and-peck typist, I find this very distracting, as it keeps me from instantly recognizing many of the keys.

I would rate this keyboard with five stars, if not for the inadequate "user guide" and the poorly thought-out keycap font.

reviews.title: Very well built
reviews.text: Solid keys, good feel.  I don't need all of the LEDS, and had to play around with the controls to get just the minimum lighting.  Much better than my old "mushy" wireless keyboard.

title: Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Clear Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)
features: Cherry MX Clear Switches - Medium Tactile and Light Clicky. They are ideal for people who want to use their keyboard for both gaming and typing.

Compact and Portable - Enjoy the freedom of wirelessness with a 2.4G connection. Reliable and fast connects with devices. It saves desk space and fits right in the backpack to carry around. Perfect for home, work and on the go.
Full Keys Anti-ghosting - All 71 keys are conflict free N-Key rollover with no input error for ultimate Gaming performance, it’s more friendly to users, ensuring smooth gaming experience.
Wireless keyboard - Simply connect your portable keyboard with one USB receiver without any software.

PBT Dye Sublimation - With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 71-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.

reviews.title: Only the 3 switches above the arrow keys are hotswapable
reviews.text:
Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Brown Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)

This Keyboard is weird. I don't know why they made only the 3 switches above the arrow key hotswapable and not the others. I like the customization of the layout, but c'mon. The keyboard sounds terrible and with the limited tuning I was able to do, it sounds and feels a bit better. The wireless works fine and doesn't lose connection. The included keycaps are not great either. I replaced those straight away. If you are looking for a keyboard to mod this is not it.

reviews.title: Compact and functional at a premium price
reviews.text:
The Ganss ALT 71 keyboard is good, but not good enough to justify the price tag. I do really like that it's wireless--the dongle works well and I didn't experience any lag while typing for work or gaming. The keyboard even came with batteries, which was great! The wired option works well, too.

I didn't love the keycaps and replaced them right away with some that I already had, which are more smooth and have a broader key surface. That's a personal preference thing, though.

I've never used clear switches and was excited to give them a try. I usually use red switches, and found the clear switches to be very comfortable. They offer more resistance and shorter travel than red switches. Once I switched to lower profile key caps, I found the noise level to be slightly quieter as well. There is a bit of spring squeak when typing, though. There are also no feet on the keyboard to adjust the height/angle of your typing surface.

Only three of the switches can be removed, though I cannot figure out why you would... It removes functionality (why would you want a page up key, but not page down?). The only reason to do this that I could think of was to more distinctly isolate the arrow keys for gaming.

The keyboard came with a keycap puller and I was able to replace all of the caps without too much trouble. The keyboard is nice and compact and I do like that it's wireless. But for the price, it's very strange that there's no backlighting or truly hot-swappable switches.

reviews.title: Cherry Switches, dual USB/wireless, but missing other features
reviews.text:
I read the specifications several times for the Ganss keyboard, thinking I had missed something. Some things, in fact. The basic product -- the experience of typing on it -- is solid, and it's good to have both wireless and wired alternatives in the same keyboard.

But . . .

At this price point, you are going to do without several features that you might want. See CON.

But what you do get is a compact (no numeric pad, no function key row) keyboard whose touch I would judge to be halfway between gamer ("clacky") and laptop membrane-like (near-silent, minimal key feedback, short throw). The keys are durable, rollover-tolerant, likely top of the line at the key level.

PRO
- Compact
- Durable, with keytop replacement tool supplied
- Well-considered "middle feel" tactile design
- Status lights are below the keyboard, which makes them more visible when accidentally set (e.g., caps lock)
- Molded plastic cover will probably last awhile

CON
- Better keep the supplied manual (an annotated 2-pp diagram), as you may not be able to figure out key combinations after not using this for awhile. Function key access, for instance, is anything but intuitive
- Limited programmability
- No USB hub/auxiliary port (for mouse)
- Not backlit (requires different model from this maker)
- Tilt feature I usually use didn't work well for me YMMV
- I miss having an alternate ENTER key on the right edge somewhere
- The DEL key is oddly placed

Despite these limitations, you might like this model for the feel.

reviews.title: There are Much Better Options for the Price
reviews.text: This board was okay but at $100 it's hard to justify it's price. The build quality is similar to your run of the mill gk61 or sk61, so it's a plastic tray mount case with an some kind of metal plate not sure if it's steel or aluminum. The board isn't hotswap aside from 3 keys in the nav cluster on the right side of the board that can be covered with a plastic plate. I personally think Ganss was really brave to offer Cherry MX Clears as an option as those bottom out at nearly 100g but I'm here for it. The acoustics of the board are pretty bad this thing is loud and I would not recommend it for an office environment unless you really hate your coworkers. The stabilizers were sufficiently lubed and fit well in the plate so they didn't rattle. The keycaps seem like a cheap dye sub set and weren't terrible. The board lacks any kind of backlighting which I find strange as it has north facing switches which is usually the reason to have them that way otherwise they can interference with row 3 of cherry profile keycaps. The board has Bluetooth but doesn't have a rechargeable battery included, just 2 AAA Duracell batteries which I will give them credit for as Duracell is a good brand. Overall the board doesn't really do anything to justify it's steep price point there are much better boards in it's price range that include better switches, backlighting, and way better acoustics.

reviews.title: Compact, Well-Made
reviews.text:
This super-compact mechanical keyboard has a solid feel for a plastic keyboard at this price point. The travel and build quality of the keys feel great, and as expected for MX Clears (which are softer than other Cherry MX switches I have used). Having a detachable USB-C cable plus wireless is the best of all worlds for connectivity and long-term usability, and should be the standard for all keyboards moving forward.

I prefer a larger footprint for my daily driver, but this is a great keyboard if you need portability or work in a small space, and still want the benefits of Cherry MX switches.

n.b. The keyboard is not backlit, which increased battery life, but definitely reduces its "gamer chic" vibe.

title: Doohoeek Updated Retro Typewriter Keyboard with White Lighting, Muti-Device Multi-OS Bluetooth & Wired Mechanical Keyboard for Laptops, Vintage Keyboard with Cradle for Mac OS/iOS/Windows/Android
features: New Version for More Flexibility: The 2022 updated typewriter keyboard is compatible with more operating system (MacOS and iOS) and also simplify the connectivity and switch process with easy connect switch button.

Retro Typewriter Style Design: The wood finished surface, retro round keys and typewriter-like looks provide good balance between functional keyboard and vintage decoration. Best decors and functional keyboard for your office or home setups.

Multi-OS with Easy Switch Button: The keyboard is compatible with Mac OS, iOS, Windows and Android operating system and only simply tap on the switch button at the top right corner to change the configiration.

Pair up to 4 Devices with Seamless Switch: The keyboard supports 3 Bluetooth and 1 wired connectivity. Only simply press switch button at the top right corner to switch between Bluetooth and wired mode. (For more details about Bluetooth connectivity please scroll down to product description)
Responsive and Fluid Keystrokes for Heavy Users: All key anti-ghosting and lifespan of over 50 million clicks provide fluid and responsive typing experience for writers, gamers, coders, etc.

Beautiful White Lighting Effects: The various lighting effects allow you to type day or night while decorating your desktop setups . Only need to fiddle with the “return” bar and left-hand knob to change lighting effects and its brightness.

Long Lasting Battery: With 2000mah rechargeable battery only about 6 hours charging the keyboard has 16-hour continuouse working time (backlighting on). Note: Working time may vary based on use and computer conditions.

What You Get: 1 x Retro Typewriter Keyboard, 2 x Blue Switch, 1 x Metal Switch Puller, 1 x USB-C Cable for Charging/Connection, 1 x User Manual; 12-month warranty & a ready support team for any issues.

reviews.title: LOVE IT
reviews.text: I absolutely 💯 ♥️ love this keyboard! It reminds me of typing class in high school. If you want nostalgia this is your product. It does make a very satisfying loud clicking when typing so do not buy if you need something quiet. Now my sister wants one too!

reviews.title: I am in love
reviews.text: I never thought I can fall in love with a keyboard! This keyboard is esthetically beautiful. I love this clicking sound…not quite like the real typewriter but kinda close:)  Light effects make typing even more fun

reviews.title: Beautiful keyboard
reviews.text: The keyboard is absolutely unique and stunning, I just received it yesterday so I have not typed on it very much but it seems pretty heavy duty and the back lighting is awesome especially with bad eyesight, The return arm regulates the lights to make it very smooth to adjust, The typewriter knobs actually do the volume on your computer so all in all I do love the keyboard, The wireless feature actually was pretty easy to set up just read the instructions carefully!  I have never spent more than $40 on a keyboard and wireless mouse but I have to tell you well worth it.

reviews.title: nice build. aesthetically interesting looking.
reviews.text: I tried to return this thinking it has connectivity issues but troubleshot my way to realize it was OS messin' with me.  Glad I gave it a another whirl because it's really nice!  one thing it does do is the lower keys next to the space button are all the same height and my fat lower palm always hits them and sends me to the beginning of the sentence I'm writing.  Annoying and somewhat dangerous for my line of work but I solved it by putting elevation risers at the front raising it approximately another 8-10 degrees.  Would have been nice if it had elevation standoffs as standard equipment.  bluetooth on this is fantastic and responsive.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming

title: EPOMAKER Macaron 1.8m Coiled Type-C to USB A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable with Detachable Aviator Connector for Gaming Keyboard/Tablet/Smart Phone (Purple)
features: 【Epomaker Macaron Cable For Keyboards and More】 To provide more options of cables, Epomaker custom Macaron cable is designed to match your keyboard, no matter what type of setup you’re working with. Chose from our different color options, it comes easy for you to match your keyboard and top off your setup. Besides that, this cable features all you want and need: Double-shielded USB 2.0 compliant, 61-gauge, 4-conductor, etc.

【Durable and Flexible】Compared with EPOMAKER MX cable, the Macaron cable adopts more softer material thus you may get a confortable experience. They are both durable and flexible due to the strict selection of the durable PP double-sleeves raw materials. This structure enables the cable to be protected from fracture. The coiled design allows the cables easily to integrate into a wide range of spaces

【USB-C Interface, USB 2.0/3.0 Compatible】For versatile connectivity, each one is equipped with a detachable aviator port to accommodate different connectors. Included in this run is a USB-C connector cable to fit modern boards

【Six Colors Choices】The cable is designed in various light colors, inspiring from the dessert Macaron, to match different keyboard trend: yellow, light green, blue, pink and etc. Epomaker custom Macaron cable adds more vibrant energy to your daily life and work. Get it to refresh your keyboard right away!

reviews.title: It looks so much cooler!
reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

features: MUST HAVE- Velocifire mechanical keyboard is designed for copywriter, programmer, editors who spend many hours typing every day. You will get better at typing speed and accuracy through Velocifire mechanical keyboards.

Durable Brown Switch- Low-noise switches have a tactile bump for feedback with every key-press. It is much QUIETER than Blue Switch but LOUDER than a regular Membrane keyboard. If you never used a mechanical keyboard before, please think about whether you can accept its sound first.

Compact Design- The compact mechanical keyboard helps save desktop space.Tiny size of compact keyboard makes it possible to share room with a mouse. Users like to have to mouse close to keyboard, since that will be easier to reach.It also serves as a accessory of laptop.

ANTI-GHOSTING - This is sometime used in place of Key Rollover, but the terminology is incorrect since ghosting is a different (and rare) issue. You will need to do more research to see what key rollover technology is being used. Ghosting is when two or more keys are pressed and a key that was not pressed registers instead.
Money Back -- Velocifire offers a 30 days money back. We always serve you after the sale!

Ultimately it could have been a nice keyboard but it fell short.

reviews.title: ULTRA Smooth, ZERO scratch and SAVES your RGBs, what else do you want?
reviews.text: These are so smooth you wont even believe it until you put it up to your ear and will hear nothing going on on the inside of the switch. After trying the Oil Kings and being super impressed, I said fine lets try the CJ Dark Blue Lubed switch and see what the fuss is about and I am glad I did. It is the end game switch. Stop the search right here. These are dead smooth a 10 out of 10, OK are about 7-10 and the Gateron Red Pro are somewhere like 5-10. They are all hard to tell and distinguish between the smoothness but once you test them out to your ear/finger you will be able to tell. I don't believe you need to lube these because well, they come lubed and since they are so smooth I don't see a need. Also the benefit with this switch is that this CJ Dark Blue comes with a slot for the LED to shine through just fine, so you will save your RGB as I have and I am glad.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

reviews.title: Nice looking and a great add for keyboard enthusiasts!
reviews.text:
Do you need a cable with an aviator connection? No, but do you want one? Yes! I like the mesh sleave on the cable, that gives a premium look and a perceived ruggedness. I chose the grey so whatever keyboard I hook up (or any USB-c peripheral) it looks nice.

Only ding I would give it is that if the keyboard needs to be to the left the cable off the coils is on top and does not look as nice. If the cable is oriented to the right, it looks better. I don't have any major issues here, but it is something that shoudl be in the product description maybe.

Overall, really nice and glad I bought it!

title: 68 Keycap PBT OEM Height Five-Sided Sublimation Compatible with GH60 / GK64 / GK61 / 68/87/104/108 Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Switch Purple Tang Dynasty 64 Keys
features: 【Widely compatible】And this keycap set can be used with most60/ 61/64/64 keyboard designs that support left shift, Cherry MX switch mechanical gaming keyboard and Outemu, Kailh and Gateron switches, and fit most standard ANSI keyboards.

【PBT dye sublimation process】These 64-key keycaps are made of high-quality PBT material, with a matte texture and anti-grease coating, which can provide first-class durability and typing feel.Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.
【Diversification Keycap Selection】Consists of 64 keycaps and DIY keycaps, suitable for all mechanical keyboards，as well as any key switch with a cross-shaped axis，Ideal for DIY mechanical keyboard.
【Specification】PBT Keycaps designed with 6.25U space bar, the thickness is around 1.5mm,The exquisite pattern is perfectly combined with the PBT keycap.
【Commodity keycap】The product is a keycap set. The keyboard in the picture is only for displaying keycaps, not for sale.

reviews.title: Clean key caps
reviews.text: Using these key caps on a Corsair K70 pro mini wireless and they look great. They feel good and match my purple and white theme

title: HONKID Esc Keycap for Mechanical Gaming Keyboard Switches, Custom Keycaps for Esc and Tab Key with Magnetic Light Macintosh Retro Style Design (White)
features: TRIBUTE TO THE CLASSICS: The retro computer keycaps is in honor of the the classic 1984 Macintosh computers, the unique retro styling of ESC keycap and Tab keycap give you a new experience

COMPATIBILITY: Uinversal mechanical keyboard Cross interface, compatible with most MX switches as cherry MX, kailh, outemu, gateron, ect. Our custom keycaps fit most keyboards especially 60% mechanical gaming keyboard, the keycaps include ESC keycap and Tab keycap

LIGHT-TRANSMISSION DESIGN: Unique light guide design, the light transmission is brighter and more uniform. If your keyboard has a backlight function, then your light will be delivered through the keycap card, and the effect is perfect. Five classic card patterns, which can be changed at will

MAGNETIC DESIGN: Both ESC and Tab keycap have magnetic, simple and stable.You can attach the mini keyboard to any keycap, card panel of the keycap also uses the magnetic function, which makes it very convenient and fast to replace the translucent cards
DELICATE EXQUISITE: You will feels better and more delicate and comfortable,The delicate matte texture and the details of the back are enough to make you miss the first generation of MAC

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

title: NPKC Blank Thick PBT OEM Profile 108 ANSI Keycaps for MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard (Only Keycap) (Dark Blue)
description:
This link is only the keycaps,no keyboard included.The keycap loading board is only to take photo,not included in the sold package.We will pack the keycaps in our own carton.
The keycap is made of durable PBT material,the thickness is about 1.5mm Compatiability It can be used for most Cherry MX Gateron MX Kailh MX and all other MX switches keyboard. Incompatiability If the spacebar is 6u 6.5u instead of 6.25u,it can't be compatible.It's not compatible with Cosair Logitech.
It's not compatible Some old Cherry keyboard whose Spacebar Capslock back hole is different,such as 1800Some Costar stabilizer keyboard whose spacebar may be clicky for thick caps.You can adjust the wire or cut the edges of inner spacebar.This is a easy problem.If you have met this problem,just leave us message.We will help you solve it. After Service If some keys missing or broken on the way,just leave us message,we will send you missing or broken ones soon.Don't worry about it and don't leave feedback immediately.For shipping time,it's about 1-3 days for most products,the system setting time is long just to avoid special case for some products.
features: This link only sells OEM profile Keycaps,no keyboard included.There is a puller presented in package.
This keycap is made of 90% PBT.The thickness is around 1.5mm.The printed one is laser etched.The blank one is no legend
This keycap is compatible with most standard ANSI MX switches keyboard whose bottom modifier key is 1x6.25u 7x1.25u.Space is around 11.7cm.
This YMDK keycap is not compatible with Corsair Razer Keyboard..64 68 75 84 96 keyboard can't use it as well

reviews.title: High quality replacement for Das
reviews.text: I purchased these for my Das Keyboard Model S (Mac). All of the keys fit, including the spacebar, and they are much higher quality than the Das OEM keys. They feel more substantial, and the sound of the keyboard is a little different, the clacking is lower pitched.

reviews.title: Really good keycaps, using on my custom mech.
reviews.text: I would give 5 stars, but one of the keys came broken from shipping. Luckily I bought for a 60key, so I didn't need that specific one.

reviews.title: great looking keycaps
reviews.text: great looking key caps surprisingly  easy to type on blank keyboard

title: 4-Port HDMI KVM Switch – 4K 60Hz – QHD 144Hz - Audio Output and USB Sharing – Mechanical & Gaming Keyboards Supported – HKS0401A2U
description:
This 4x1 HDMI KVM Switch allows you to share one set of “keyboard, video monitor, and mouse” (“KVM”) with up to four PC’s or video devices (Xbox, PS4, Firestick, etc). The switcher does not require software and works with Windows, MacOS, Unix/Linux, Raspberri Pi, etc. HDMI 2.0 protocol supports 4K@60hz and blazing 144hz@1440P, making this device a perfect fit for work-from-home professionals and high-end video gaming.
Key Features
- Supports DisplayPort, DVI, & VGA with approved adapters.  - Keyboard passthrough mode allows for better compatibility plus multimedia key functions  - Hotkey triggers assignable to either Scroll Lock or Right-CTRL  - USB sharing works with printers, jump drives, USB headsets and webcams for teleconferencing.  - Auto-scanning with adjustable time interval from 1 to 250 seconds.  - 1-year US-based warranty when purchased from BuyTESmart
FAQ
- Audio is transmitted via HDMI connection. HDMI or DisplayPort monitor required for audio  - Use powered USB hub to expand the USB2.0 port if multiple connections are needed  - Use USB extender to reduce distance to receiver if wireless mouse/keyboard is “jumpy”  - Please install CR2025 battery before use (battery not included)
Mouse / Keyboard Compatibility
Dedicated mouse & keyboard ports have emulation programming. Second gen chipsets have “Passthrough Mode” to send special characters such as multimedia keys (play, pause, volume up/down), which improves keyboard compatibility. Keyboard software such as Logitech Options, Corsair iCUE, etc conflicts with the emulation and limits the ability to use macro keys. We recommend using the KVM’s USB2.0 port as a workaround if this software if needed. USB2.0 port does not have emulation programming and KVM will not recognize hotkey commands. Please use a powered USB hub to expand USB connectivity.
features: Share 4 computers with 1 monitor, keyboard, mouse. Switch between PC’s with push button, keyboard hotkeys, mouse gestures, or remote control. Can also use as HDMI switch for gaming consoles and streaming devices.
HDMI 2.0 supports UHD 4K60hz (3840x2160), QHD 144hz (2560x1440), 1080P 144hz (1920x1080). Plus Ultrawide 60hz (21:9 & 32:9). HDCP 2.2, HDR10, Dolby Vision. EDID Emulation.
Plug & Play. No software or drivers needed. Supported by IT departments for work-from-home professionals. Separate your work from your personal life without the hassle & clutter!
Transmit audio to monitor via HDMI and/or external speakers via stereo output. Share USB device such as webcam and headset with dedicated USB2.0 port. Great for Zoom and Twitch users.
Supports wired, wireless, mechanical, multimedia, and gaming keyboards. No delay, zero latency keyboard switching and pass-through mode for multimedia keys. READ FAQ & PICS.
U.S. Based TECHNICAL SUPPORT from BuyTESmart available to help resolve all issues.

reviews.title: Mostly good
reviews.text: One of my connected PCs has a hard time getting the resolution to 1440, cannot figure out why.  I have to restart to get it to go from 1080 to 1440.

reviews.title: Great KVM switch
reviews.text: I wasn’t sure what to expect , but after having it for a week I really like the switch.  Perfect size and functionality.

title: Matcha Keycaps Set 124 Keys XDA PBT Keycaps Dye Sublimation Fit for 61/64/87/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches Mechanical Keyboard
features: 【Note 】Please know before placing an order that this is a set of keycaps, not a keyboard. If the keycaps missing or broken, please contact customer service to send you new keys.
【PBT keycaps】These green keycaps are made of thick PBT material, and the surface of the keycaps has an oil-repellent coating, which can provide you with a good typing experience.
【XDA Keycaps】These Matcha keycaps are XDA profile, and each keycap has the same height. The top of the keycap is relatively wide, which can bring you a comfortable typing experience.

【Wide Compatibility】This set of matcha keycaps has a total of 124 keys, suitable for 61/87/100/104/108 cherry mx switches mechanical keyboard. If you are not sure whether it is suitable for your keyboard, please contact customer service.
【Simple Installation】In order to facilitate you to install this set of keycaps, we have equipped you with a key puller to facilitate you to remove the keycaps from the keyboard.

reviews.title: Nice keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my Anne Pro case without issue. Nice colors and easy to type with.

reviews.title: Great for the price!
reviews.text: This is a clone set as far as I'm aware, but I don't mind that. The arrows are cuter than the "original" Matcha set that can be found for the same price. These feel great, sound great, look great. I couldn't be happier!

reviews.title: these are sweeeet
reviews.text: They're really good quality for the price! Very retro. Use them to replace the ugly keychron ones you get with a keyboard.

reviews.text: My keyboard only has like 64 keys so most of them I don’t use. But they are great, easy to put in. Fits my desk theme very well :))
